title: CapabilityToken
description: Scoped, attenuable authority passed from an outer ring to an inner ring. Inner rings MAY narrow (attenuate) the token before delegating further; they MUST NOT widen it. Modeled on macaroons: authority = caveats, not identity.

token_id.description: Unique id for this token (useful for revocation and audit).

subject.description: agent_id the token is issued to. Only this subject may present it.

capabilities.description: The set of permitted actions. Empty array means the token grants nothing — reject such tokens.

scope.description: Resource selectors that narrow the action (e.g., {'thread_id': 't_123'}, {'tenant': 'acme'}).

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp after which the token MUST be rejected.

attenuated_from.description: token_id of the parent token this one narrows. Enables auditing the delegation chain.

nonce.description: Random value to prevent replay. Producers SHOULD track seen nonces per subject.

signature.description: Issuer's signature over the canonical serialization of this token (excluding the signature field). Verifiers reject tokens whose signature doesn't match the issuer's known key.
